real: The HTML demo uses committed compiler, runtime, and validation artifacts.
The interactive LLM path can use a local HuggingFace Qwen model when optional dependencies and model files are available.
not_claimed: The live camera / CV detection slot is not connected in this demo.
The demo does not run production vLLM, SGLang, Triton, TensorRT-LLM, or live CV inference.

scenarios.label: Long Context
scenarios.default_question: Summarize the incident and list the highest-risk follow-up items.

context_items.text: Deployment changed request batching from fixed groups to continuous decode scheduling.
Queue wait improved, but KV cache pressure increased during long prompts.
Validation requires TTFT, TPOT, memory budget, and correctness checks.
The next review needs a concise operator-facing action list.

task_context.title: Runtime incident review
task_context.subtitle: Summarize scheduling and memory pressure evidence.

scenarios.label: Instruction Rewrite
scenarios.default_question: Rewrite this into a clear production runbook instruction.

context_items.text: Check model readiness before admitting traffic.
If TTFT exceeds the SLO, inspect prefill queue depth and cache hit rate.
Keep rollback instructions short enough for an on-call handoff.
Do not claim root cause until validation evidence is attached.

task_context.title: Runbook rewrite
task_context.subtitle: Turn rough serving notes into operator instructions.

scenarios.label: Technical Explain
scenarios.default_question: Explain how prompt lowering and runtime scheduling change TTFT and total latency.

context_items.text: BASEMODEL uses the full prompt and a larger generation budget.
Optimized runtime uses a compact prompt contract and compiler-generated serving metadata.
Runtime evidence is artifact-backed for scheduler, KV pressure, and memory policy.
Live Qwen metrics should be separated from artifact-backed policy claims.

task_context.title: Compiler runtime explanation
task_context.subtitle: Explain optimization signals without overclaiming internals.
